LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF LOUIS MARCELLI'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS OFF CALENDAR. DUPLICATE ENTRY. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. PLAINTIFF LOUIS MARCELLI'S RE-NOTICED MOTION TO TAX COSTS SOUGHT BY DEFENDANT "THE MALPASO COMPANY, INC." IS GRANTED IN PART, AND DENIED IN PART. MALPASO COMPANY INC. WAS THE PREVAILING PARTY IN THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION. THE MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED AS TO $853.52 IN MESSENGER COSTS TO FILE DOCUMENTS WITH THE COURT. THIS IS NOT SERVICE OF PROCESS PURSUANT TO CCP 1033.5(A)(4), AND THE COURT DECLINES TO EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO AWARD THESE PARTICULAR COSTS UNDER CCP 1033.5(C)(4) BASED ON THE SHOWING BEFORE ME. THE MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS GRANTED AS TO THE COSTS OF THE DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF. THERE IS INSUFFICIENT PROOF THAT DEFENDANT NOTICED THIS DEPOSITION OR INCURRED ANY COST FOR IT; EXHIBIT K TO THE BENBOW DECLARATION IS AN INVOICE FOR $38.00. AS TO THE OTHER DEPOSITION COSTS, DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO MEET ITS EVIDENTIARY BURDEN TO SHOW THAT THIS DEFENDANT ISSUED THESE DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS, AND WHAT THE ITEMS ON THESE INVOICES (EXHIBIT L) ARE INTENDED TO COVER. THE MOTION TO TAX COSTS IS THUS GRANTED AS TO ALL OF THE "DEPOSITION" CHARGES IN THE AMOUNT OF $2069.82. THE TOTAL AMOUNT OF COSTS TAXED (I.E., DEDUCTED) IS $2905.34. THE COURT WILL REVIEW AND SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER THAT WAS EMAILED TO THE COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Re Ntc Of Pltf Louis Marcelli'S Motiopn To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Jan-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF LOUIS MARCELLI's Re-noticed Motion To Tax Costs sought by Defendant "The Malpaso Company, Inc." is granted in part, and denied in part. Malpaso Company Inc. was the prevailing party in the summary judgment motion. The motion to tax costs is granted as to $853.52 in messenger costs to file documents with the Court. This is not service of process pursuant to CCP 1033.5(a)(4), and the Court declines to exercise its discretion to award these particular costs under CCP 1033.5(c)(4) based on the showing before me. The motion to tax costs is granted as to the costs of the deposition of plaintiff. There is insufficient proof that Defendant noticed this deposition or incurred any cost for it; Exhibit K to the Benbow declaration is an invoice for $38.00. As to the other deposition costs, Defendant has failed to meet its evidentiary burden to show that this Defendant issued these deposition subpoenas, and what the items on these invoices (Exhibit L) are intended to cover. The motion to tax costs is thus granted as to all of the "deposition" charges in the amount of $2069.82. The total amount of costs taxed (i.e., deducted) is $2905.34. The Court will review and sign the proposed order that was emailed to the court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
